proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine`.
Patients with `treatment_7plus3` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without `treatment_7plus3`.

Patients with any FLT3/IDH targeted treatment (`treatment_midostaurin`, `treatment_gilteritinib`, `treatment_ivosidenib`, or `treatment_enasidenib`) have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without that class of treatment.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.0% with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs 16.6% without it; risk difference 5.4% (p=1.00e-300).
Response was 18.9% with treatment_7plus3 vs 18.6% without it; risk difference 0.3% (p=0.458).
Response was 18.5% with any FLT3/IDH targeted treatment vs 18.9% without it; risk difference -0.4% (p=0.274).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `tp53_mutation` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `tp53_mutation`.
Patients with `complex_karyotype` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `complex_karyotype`.
Patients with `npm1_mutation` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without `npm1_mutation`.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.6% with tp53_mutation vs 19.0% without it; risk difference -2.4% (p=1.43e-5).
Response was 16.8% with complex_karyotype vs 19.1% without it; risk difference -2.3% (p=1.34e-6).
Response was 23.5% with npm1_mutation vs 16.7% without it; risk difference 6.7% (p=1.00e-300).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `unfit_for_intensive` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients without `unfit_for_intensive`.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `age_years` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `ecog_ps` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each 10-year increase in `age_years` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 22.1% among unfit_for_intensive patients vs 16.5% among others; risk difference 5.6% (p=1.00e-300).
A 1-SD increase in age_years (10.9 years) changed response probability by -0.0% (p=0.882).
A 1-SD increase in ecog_ps (0.68 points) changed response probability by -3.5% (p=1.00e-300).

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with age centered and scaled per 10 years.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `age_years` per 10 years was -0.00073 with p=0.6439, showing no meaningful independent age effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `flt3_itd`, `treatment_midostaurin` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_midostaurin`.
The positive association of `treatment_midostaurin` with `objective_response` is stronger in `flt3_itd` patients than in non-`flt3_itd` patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within flt3_itd patients, response was 17.6% with treatment_midostaurin vs 19.1% without it; risk difference -1.5% (p=0.167).
Observed interaction risk difference was -1.6%: midostaurin RD -1.5% in flt3_itd vs 0.1% in non-flt3_itd patients (p=0.187).

analyses.code: adjusted_lpm(objective_response ~ treatment_midostaurin * flt3_itd + age + sex + ecog + secondary_aml + unfit + complex_karyotype + tp53)
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, the treatment_midostaurin by flt3_itd interaction was -1.7% on the response-probability scale (p=0.164).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `flt3_tkd`, `treatment_gilteritinib` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_gilteritinib`.
The positive association of `treatment_gilteritinib` with `objective_response` is stronger in `flt3_tkd` patients than in non-`flt3_tkd` patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within flt3_tkd patients, response was 15.1% with treatment_gilteritinib vs 18.6% without it; risk difference -3.5% (p=0.0691).
Observed interaction risk difference was -2.9%: gilteritinib RD -3.5% in flt3_tkd vs -0.6% in non-flt3_tkd patients (p=0.140).
After adjustment, the treatment_gilteritinib by flt3_tkd interaction was -3.1% (p=0.115).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `idh1_mutation`, `treatment_ivosidenib` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_ivosidenib`.
Among patients with `idh2_mutation`, `treatment_enasidenib` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_enasidenib`.

analyses.result_summary: Within idh1_mutation patients, response was 15.4% with treatment_ivosidenib vs 19.4% without it; risk difference -4.0% (p=0.0703).
The observed treatment_ivosidenib by idh1_mutation interaction was -4.4% (p=0.0569).
Within idh2_mutation patients, response was 19.5% with treatment_enasidenib vs 19.1% without it; risk difference 0.4% (p=0.866).
The observed treatment_enasidenib by idh2_mutation interaction was 0.6% (p=0.791).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with `unfit_for_intensive`, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine`.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is stronger in `unfit_for_intensive` patients than in fit patients.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `tp53_mutation` than in patients without `tp53_mutation`.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with low `albumin_g_dl` at or below the cohort first quartile.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with high `blast_pct_marrow` at or above the cohort third quartile.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with high `wbc_k_per_ul` at or above the cohort third quartile.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with high `weight_loss_pct_6mo` at or above the cohort third quartile.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with high `crp_mg_l` at or above the cohort third quartile.

analyses.result_summary: Within unfit_for_intensive patients, response was 30.8% with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs 16.3% without it; risk difference 14.5% (p=1.00e-300).
Observed interaction risk difference was 15.3%: venetoclax/azacitidine RD 14.5% in unfit vs -0.8% in fit patients (p=1.00e-300).
After adjustment, the treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine by unfit_for_intensive interaction was 15.3% (p=1.00e-300).

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model plus stratified response-rate comparisons.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `tp53_mutation` by `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` interaction was -0.07178. Stratified unadjusted treatment differences were +6.00 percentage points in TP53-wildtype patients and -0.36 percentage points in TP53-mutated patients.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with low-albumin interaction term.
analyses.result_summary: Using low albumin defined as `albumin_g_dl` <= 3.5, the adjusted low-albumin by venetoclax-azacitidine interaction was -0.01466.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: High blast burden was defined as `blast_pct_marrow` >= 73.3. The interaction with venetoclax-azacitidine was +0.01163 and not significant.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: High WBC was defined as `wbc_k_per_ul` >= 24.1. The interaction with venetoclax-azacitidine was +0.00220 and not significant.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: High weight loss was defined as `weight_loss_pct_6mo` >= 6.4. The interaction with venetoclax-azacitidine was -0.00487 and not significant.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: High CRP was defined as `crp_mg_l` >= 7.04. The interaction with venetoclax-azacitidine was -0.00948 and not significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients without `unfit_for_intensive`, `treatment_7plus3` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_7plus3`.
The positive association of `treatment_7plus3` with `objective_response` is weaker in `unfit_for_intensive` patients than in fit patients.
The association of `treatment_7plus3` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `complex_karyotype` than in patients without `complex_karyotype`.
The association of `treatment_7plus3` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `unfit_for_intensive` than in patients without `unfit_for_intensive`.
The association of `treatment_7plus3` with `objective_response` is larger in patients with `npm1_mutation` than in patients without `npm1_mutation`.
The association of `treatment_7plus3` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients aged 75 years or older than in younger patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within fit patients, response was 16.7% with treatment_7plus3 vs 16.3% without it; risk difference 0.4% (p=0.368).
Observed interaction risk difference was -0.4%: 7plus3 RD 0.0% in unfit vs 0.4% in fit patients (p=0.609).
After adjustment, the treatment_7plus3 by unfit_for_intensive interaction was -0.3% (p=0.724).

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model plus stratified response-rate comparisons.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `complex_karyotype` by `treatment_7plus3` interaction was -0.03281. Unadjusted treatment difference was -2.32 percentage points in complex karyotype disease and +0.72 points without complex karyotype.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `unfit_for_intensive` by `treatment_7plus3` interaction was -0.00240 and not significant.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `npm1_mutation` by `treatment_7plus3` interaction was -0.01261 with p=0.1018, not supporting larger benefit in NPM1-mutated patients.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted age >=75 by `treatment_7plus3` interaction was +0.00807 and not significant, opposite the hypothesized smaller benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `nlr` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each 1 g/dL increase in `albumin_g_dl` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability after adjustment.
Each 5 percentage-point increase in `weight_loss_pct_6mo` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.
Each 5 mg/L increase in `crp_mg_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.
Each 1-unit increase in `nlr` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: A 1-SD increase in albumin_g_dl (0.50 g/dL) changed response probability by 0.9% (p=2.82e-7).
A 1-SD increase in crp_mg_l (9.2 mg/L) changed response probability by -0.8% (p=6.04e-6).
A 1-SD increase in nlr (2.10) changed response probability by -0.0% (p=0.919).

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model plus quartile contrast.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `albumin_g_dl` per 1 g/dL was +0.01810. Highest versus lowest quartile response was 19.78% versus 17.53%.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model plus quartile contrast.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `weight_loss_pct_6mo` per 5 percentage points was -0.02597. Highest versus lowest quartile response was 15.72% versus 20.46%.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model plus quartile contrast.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `crp_mg_l` per 5 mg/L was -0.00424. Highest versus lowest quartile response was 17.22% versus 19.46%.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `nlr` per 1 unit was +0.00020 with p=0.8131, refuting a negative independent association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `wbc_k_per_ul` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `blast_pct_marrow` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `ldh_u_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each 10 percentage-point increase in `blast_pct_marrow` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.
Each 10 k/uL increase in `wbc_k_per_ul` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.
Each 100 U/L increase in `ldh_u_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: A 1-SD increase in wbc_k_per_ul (26.0 k/uL) changed response probability by -0.7% (p=1.82e-4).
A 1-SD increase in blast_pct_marrow (19.2 percentage points) changed response probability by -1.4% (p=2.22e-16).
A 1-SD increase in ldh_u_l (80.9 U/L) changed response probability by -0.2% (p=0.350).

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model plus quartile contrast.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `blast_pct_marrow` per 10 percentage points was -0.00766. In an unadjusted quartile contrast, the highest quartile had 16.64% response versus 20.65% in the lowest quartile.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model plus quartile contrast.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `wbc_k_per_ul` per 10 k/uL was -0.00255. Highest versus lowest quartile response was 17.60% versus 19.89%.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `ldh_u_l` per 100 U/L was -0.00186 with p=0.3826, not statistically significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `secondary_aml` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `secondary_aml`.
Among patients with `secondary_aml`, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine`.
The positive association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is stronger in `secondary_aml` patients than in de novo AML patients.
Patients with `secondary_aml` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `secondary_aml` after adjustment.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.8% with secondary_aml vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.1% (p=0.841).
Within secondary_aml patients, response was 22.1% with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs 16.6% without it; risk difference 5.5% (p=4.91e-14).
Observed interaction risk difference was 0.2%: venetoclax/azacitidine RD 5.5% in secondary_aml vs 5.3% in de novo AML (p=0.792).

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with heteroskedasticity-robust standard errors.
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted coefficient for `secondary_aml` was +0.00043 with p=0.9139, providing no evidence of lower response after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with either `tp53_mutation` or `complex_karyotype` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients with neither adverse feature.
Patients with both `tp53_mutation` and `complex_karyotype` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without both features.
The adverse association of `tp53_mutation` with `objective_response` is stronger in patients with `complex_karyotype` than in patients without `complex_karyotype`.
The positive `npm1_mutation` association with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `tp53_mutation` than in patients without `tp53_mutation`.
The positive `npm1_mutation` association with `objective_response` is smaller in patients with `complex_karyotype` than in patients without `complex_karyotype`.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 16.8% with tp53_mutation or complex_karyotype vs 19.4% with neither; risk difference -2.6% (p=8.13e-11).
Response was 16.0% with both tp53_mutation and complex_karyotype vs 18.8% otherwise; risk difference -2.8% (p=0.0375).
Observed interaction risk difference was 1.8%: tp53 RD -0.9% in complex_karyotype vs -2.7% without complex_karyotype (p=0.237).

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with interaction term.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `npm1_mutation` by `tp53_mutation` interaction was -0.07697, consistent with TP53-mutated disease attenuating the NPM1-associated response advantage.

analyses.method: Adjusted linear probability model with interaction term.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `npm1_mutation` by `complex_karyotype` interaction was -0.09696, indicating strong attenuation of the NPM1 response advantage in complex karyotype disease.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients without `flt3_itd`, `npm1_mutation` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `npm1_mutation`.
The favorable association of `npm1_mutation` with `objective_response` is weaker in `flt3_itd` patients than in non-`flt3_itd` patients.

analyses.result_summary: Within flt3_itd-negative patients, response was 23.4% with npm1_mutation vs 16.7% without it; risk difference 6.8% (p=1.00e-300).
Observed interaction risk difference was -0.2%: npm1 RD 6.6% in flt3_itd vs 6.8% in flt3_itd-negative patients (p=0.832).
After adjustment, the npm1_mutation by flt3_itd interaction was -0.3% (p=0.772).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients without `flt3_itd`, `treatment_midostaurin` is not associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_midostaurin`.
Among patients without `idh1_mutation`, `treatment_ivosidenib` is not associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_ivosidenib`.
Among patients without `idh2_mutation`, `treatment_enasidenib` is not associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_enasidenib`.

analyses.result_summary: Within flt3_itd-negative patients, response was 18.8% with treatment_midostaurin vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.1% (p=0.854).
Within idh1_mutation-negative patients, response was 19.1% with treatment_ivosidenib vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.4% (p=0.553).
Within idh2_mutation-negative patients, response was 18.5% with treatment_enasidenib vs 18.7% without it; risk difference -0.2% (p=0.725).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among patients with either `flt3_itd` or `flt3_tkd`, `treatment_gilteritinib` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_gilteritinib`.
The positive association of `treatment_gilteritinib` with `objective_response` is stronger in patients with either `flt3_itd` or `flt3_tkd` than in patients with neither FLT3 alteration.

analyses.result_summary: Within patients with either flt3_itd or flt3_tkd, response was 18.2% with treatment_gilteritinib vs 18.8% without it; risk difference -0.6% (p=0.542).
Observed interaction risk difference was 0.2%: gilteritinib RD -0.6% in FLT3-altered vs -0.8% in FLT3-wild-type patients (p=0.863).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with either `idh1_mutation` or `idh2_mutation` have a higher `objective_response` rate than patients with neither IDH mutation.
Among patients with either `idh1_mutation` or `idh2_mutation`, `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` is associated with higher `objective_response` than no `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine`.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 19.1% with idh1_mutation or idh2_mutation vs 18.7% with neither; risk difference 0.5% (p=0.340).
Within IDH-mutated patients, response was 22.3% with treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine vs 17.0% without it; risk difference 5.3% (p=3.02e-9).
Observed venetoclax/azacitidine by IDH-any interaction was -0.0% (p=0.960).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `hemoglobin_g_dl` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `platelets_k_ul` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `creatinine_mg_dl` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.

analyses.result_summary: A 1-SD increase in hemoglobin_g_dl (1.79 g/dL) changed response probability by -0.3% (p=0.150).
A 1-SD increase in platelets_k_ul (69.6 k/uL) changed response probability by -0.1% (p=0.484).
A 1-SD increase in creatinine_mg_dl (0.30 mg/dL) changed response probability by -0.1% (p=0.716).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `chronic_kidney_disease` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `chronic_kidney_disease`.
Patients with `heart_failure` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `heart_failure`.
Patients with `diabetes_mellitus` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `diabetes_mellitus`.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 19.5% with chronic_kidney_disease vs 18.6% without it; risk difference 0.9% (p=0.0982).
Response was 18.5% with heart_failure vs 18.8% without it; risk difference -0.3% (p=0.602).
Response was 18.9% with diabetes_mellitus vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.1% (p=0.745).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, and TP53 status, `npm1_mutation` remains positively associated with `objective_response`.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, and TP53 status, `tp53_mutation` remains negatively associated with `objective_response`.
After adjustment for age, sex, ECOG, secondary AML, fitness, complex karyotype, and TP53 status, `complex_karyotype` remains negatively associated with `objective_response`.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted npm1_mutation association was 6.8% on the response-probability scale (p=1.00e-300).
Adjusted tp53_mutation association was -2.5% on the response-probability scale (p=6.06e-6).
Adjusted complex_karyotype association was -2.4% on the response-probability scale (p=5.30e-7).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Solid-tumor marker `her2_amplification` is not associated with higher `objective_response` in this AML cohort.
Solid-tumor marker `met_exon14_skipping` is not associated with higher `objective_response` in this AML cohort.
Solid-tumor marker `ros1_fusion` is not associated with higher `objective_response` in this AML cohort.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.1% with her2_amplification vs 18.8% without it; risk difference -0.6% (p=0.533).
Response was 18.9% with met_exon14_skipping vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.1% (p=0.917).
Response was 19.4% with ros1_fusion vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.7% (p=0.703).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `pleural_effusion` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `pleural_effusion`.
Patients with `pericardial_effusion` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `pericardial_effusion`.
Patients with `liver_mets` have a lower `objective_response` rate than patients without `liver_mets`.

analyses.result_summary: Response was 18.6% with pleural_effusion vs 18.8% without it; risk difference -0.1% (p=0.791).
Response was 19.9% with pericardial_effusion vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 1.2% (p=0.178).
Response was 19.3% with liver_mets vs 18.7% without it; risk difference 0.6% (p=0.203).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `heart_rate_bpm` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `spo2_pct` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `bmi` is associated with a higher `objective_response` probability.

A 1-SD increase in spo2_pct (1.98 percentage points) changed response probability by -0.2% (p=0.374).
A 1-SD increase in bmi (5.0 kg/m2) changed response probability by 0.0% (p=0.829).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Each one-standard-deviation increase in `ast_u_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `alt_u_l` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.
Each one-standard-deviation increase in `total_bilirubin_mg_dl` is associated with a lower `objective_response` probability.

analyses.result_summary: A 1-SD increase in ast_u_l (11.7 U/L) changed response probability by -0.3% (p=0.128).
A 1-SD increase in alt_u_l (12.2 U/L) changed response probability by 0.2% (p=0.195).
A 1-SD increase in total_bilirubin_mg_dl (0.32 mg/dL) changed response probability by -0.2% (p=0.216).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with `flt3_itd` are more likely to receive `treatment_midostaurin` than patients without `flt3_itd`.
Patients with `idh1_mutation` are more likely to receive `treatment_ivosidenib` than patients without `idh1_mutation`.
Patients with `idh2_mutation` are more likely to receive `treatment_enasidenib` than patients without `idh2_mutation`.
Among patients with `flt3_itd`, `treatment_midostaurin` is associated with higher `objective_response` than in FLT3-ITD patients not receiving midostaurin.
Among patients with `flt3_itd`, `treatment_gilteritinib` is associated with higher `objective_response` than in FLT3-ITD patients not receiving gilteritinib.

analyses.result_summary: Midostaurin use was 14.7% with flt3_itd vs 15.2% without flt3_itd; risk difference -0.5% (p=0.197).
Ivosidenib use was 8.2% with idh1_mutation vs 7.8% without idh1_mutation; risk difference 0.4% (p=0.429).
Enasidenib use was 7.6% with idh2_mutation vs 8.0% without idh2_mutation; risk difference -0.4% (p=0.305).

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model plus FLT3-ITD subgroup response-rate comparison.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `flt3_itd` by `treatment_midostaurin` interaction was -0.01424. In FLT3-ITD patients, unadjusted response was 17.64% on midostaurin versus 19.14% off.

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model plus FLT3-ITD subgroup response-rate comparison.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted `flt3_itd` by `treatment_gilteritinib` interaction was +0.01016 and not significant. In FLT3-ITD patients, unadjusted response difference was only +0.23 percentage points.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for baseline covariates, the `treatment_ivosidenib` by `idh1_mutation` interaction is negatively associated with `objective_response`.
After adjustment for baseline covariates, the `treatment_enasidenib` by `idh2_mutation` interaction remains positively associated with `objective_response`.
After adjustment for baseline covariates, the `treatment_gilteritinib` by `flt3_any` interaction remains positively associated with `objective_response`.
The association of `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` with `objective_response` is smaller in patients aged 75 years or older than in younger patients.

analyses.result_summary: Adjusted treatment_ivosidenib by idh1_mutation interaction was -4.9% (p=0.0318).
Adjusted treatment_enasidenib by idh2_mutation interaction was 0.7% (p=0.756).
Adjusted treatment_gilteritinib by flt3_any interaction was 0.2% (p=0.841).

analyses.method: Adjusted interaction model.
analyses.result_summary: The adjusted age >=75 by `treatment_venetoclax_azacitidine` interaction was -0.00804 with p=0.2744, not statistically significant.
